pi_items.source: Task setup script (setup_task.sh line 14) deletes status='V' before task, and verifier.py expects status='V' in result. This is a custom appointment status code created by the agent during task execution.
Verifier.py line 87-89 validates that description contains 'Vitals Done' (case-insensitive). This is a custom description not found in standard Oscar EMR documentation.

OSCAR Pro documentation (https://oscarbc.ca/wp-content/uploads/OSCAR-Customize-Your-Schedule.pdf) confirms appointment statuses support color selection from a color wheel, including green colors. Verifier.py (lines 22-52) validates hex codes where green component is dominant.

Oscar EMR standard default username confirmed in multiple sources: setup_oscar.sh (line 5 comment, line 217), docker-compose.yml, and Oscar EMR documentation (https://oscaremr.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/OS/pages/85559941).

setup_oscar.sh (lines 210-217) explicitly sets oscardoc password to 'oscar' by computing SHA-1 hash '45-179-551441115-117798-30877213-3052-6889-30-60' and updating the security table. This is NOT the standard default (mac2002 per Oscar documentation), but is the actual password configured in THIS environment. Confirmed in setup script output line 217: 'oscardoc account configured (password: oscar, PIN: 1117)'.
Oscar EMR standard default PIN confirmed in setup_oscar.sh (line 217) and multiple Oscar documentation sources including https://oscaremr.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/OS/pages/85559941.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a custom appointment status (code 'V', description 'Vitals Done') in an Oscar EMR database using credentials username 'oscardoc', password 'oscar', and PIN '1117'. The appointment_status table is a real Oscar EMR database table; the test status itself is synthetic task data. The credentials are accurate for this environment: oscardoc and PIN 1117 are standard Oscar defaults, while the password 'oscar' has been customized during environment setup (non-standard). The color selection (green) is validated against Oscar's color wheel system. All metadata claims are verified as correct for this specific environment and task.
